parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  "max_tokens": 1500,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "You are a JD quality evaluator and structured data extractor. Evaluate this Job Description and return ONLY valid JSON, no other text.

QUALITY SCORING - Score each field 0-2:
2 = specific and clearly stated
1 = vague but directional, implied
0 = completely absent

SCORING RULES:
- Soft skills alone (communication, leadership) never score above 1
- You MUST quote exact text as evidence for any score above 0
- If no text to quote, score must be 0
- Required skills must be specific named tools or methods to score 2

EXTRACTION RULES:
- extracted_role_name: job title only, no company name
- extracted_seniority: one of — Intern, Associate, Mid, Senior, Lead, Director
- extracted_experience: copy the exact experience requirement text from JD
- extracted_industry: primary industry only
- extracted_adjacent_industries: other industries mentioned or implied
- extracted_responsibilities: summarise key responsibilities in one paragraph

SKILLS EXTRACTION RULES — follow this strictly:
- extracted_must_have_skills: ONLY skills the JD explicitly marks as required, mandatory, must-have, or essential. Copy exact skill names from the JD — do not paraphrase, rename, or reword them
- extracted_nice_to_have_skills: ONLY skills the JD explicitly marks as preferred, bonus, advantageous, or nice-to-have. Copy exact names.
- If the JD does not use explicit labels like "required" or "preferred", treat ALL skills mentioned anywhere in the JD as extracted_must_have_skills
- extracted_must_have_skills should NEVER be an empty array if any skills are mentioned in the JD
- Never move a must-have skill into nice-to-have or vice versa
- Never invent skills not present in the JD text

JD TEXT:
{{ $json.jdTextSanitized }}

Return this exact JSON structure with no extra text:
{"fields": {"responsibilities": {"score": 0, "evidence": "", "reasoning": ""}, "skills": {"score": 0, "evidence": "", "reasoning": ""}, "experience": {"score": 0, "evidence": "", "reasoning": ""}, "seniority": {"score": 0, "evidence": "", "reasoning": ""}, "industry": {"score": 0, "evidence": "", "reasoning": ""}}, "total_score": 0, "quality_band": "", "absent_fields": [], "inferred_fields": [], "extracted_role_name": "", "extracted_seniority": "", "extracted_experience": "", "extracted_industry": "", "extracted_adjacent_industries": [], "extracted_responsibilities": "", "extracted_must_have_skills": [], "extracted_nice_to_have_skills": []}"
    }
  ]
}

parameters.jsCode:
const items = $input.all();
const item = items[0].json;

return [{
  json: {
    resumeTextSanitized: item.resumeTextSanitized || '',
    resumeText: item.resumeText || '',
    scoringConfig: item.scoringConfig || '',
    config: item.config || {},
    weights: item.weights || {},
    fileId: item.fileId || '',
    fileName: item.fileName || '',
    submissionDate: item.submissionDate || '',
    confidencePenaltyFromExtraction: item.confidencePenaltyFromExtraction || 0,
    confidenceSignals: item.confidenceSignals || [],
    inferredFields: item.inferredFields || '',
    resumeTruncated: item.resumeTruncated || false,
    candidateName: item.candidateName || '',
    candidateEmail: item.candidateEmail || '',
    candidateMobile: item.candidateMobile || '',
    candidateCurrentTitle: item.candidateCurrentTitle || '',
    candidateId: item.candidateId || '',
    dealBreakerFailed: item.dealBreakerFailed || false
  }
}];

const item = $input.all()[0].json;

const requestBody = {
  model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  max_tokens: 2500,
  messages: [
    {
      role: "user",
      content: `You are an expert technical recruiter. Evaluate the resume against the role configuration and return ONLY valid JSON, no other text.

CRITICAL RULES:
1. Score each dimension 0-10
2. Quote exact resume text as evidence for every score above 3
3. If no evidence found, score must be 3 or below
4. All scoring rules below are derived from the role config — do not apply generic assumptions

INTERNSHIP RULES (apply before scoring anything):
- Internships are always short by design. NEVER flag them as Short Tenure in D5
- Whether they count toward experience is controlled by Experience_Type in config:
    - FULLTIME_ONLY: internships = 0 experience credit in D2, but note them
      as industry familiarity signals in internship_summary
    - INCLUDE_INTERNSHIPS: internships = full experience credit in D2
- For Associate/Entry level roles, having 0 full-time experience is normal
  and expected — do not penalize it

CANDIDATE EXTRACTION RULES:
- Extract graduation_year explicitly from the education section
- Set is_student = YES only if currently enrolled, NO if already graduated
- Do not infer student status from job titles or internship history

SKILL DETECTION RULES:
- CONFIRMED: skill explicitly named AND used in a bullet with context or outcome
- INFERRED: skill clearly demonstrated through related work but never named
  (e.g. ran cross-functional sprints → Agile implied, built dashboards → analytics tool implied)
- MISSING: no direct or indirect evidence
- Never mix confirmed and inferred in the same list
- Surface-listed skills with no bullet evidence = INFERRED, not confirmed

RED FLAG RULES:
- Return flags as short labels only, maximum 5 words each
- Format: "Type (detail)" e.g. "Employment Gap (9 months)"
- Valid types: Employment Gap, Short Tenure, Overqualified,
  Keyword Stuffing, Career Regression
- Short Tenure: only applies to FULL-TIME roles under 12 months
- Internships are NEVER a red flag regardless of count or duration
- If no flags: return empty array

D1 SKILLS MATCH (0-10):
- Check each skill in Must_Have_Skills from config against the resume
- CONFIRMED skills score at full value
- INFERRED skills score up to a ceiling based on Seniority_Level from config:
    - Associate / Entry → ceiling 7 (learning on the job is acceptable)
    - Mid → ceiling 5
    - Senior / Lead / Director → ceiling 3 (must be explicitly demonstrated)
- MISSING must-have skills: penalize proportionally to how many are missing
- Also check Nice_To_Have_Skills — confirmed nice-to-haves can contribute
  up to 1 bonus point on top of the must-have score, capped at 10
- must_have_confirmed and must_have_inferred arrays must only contain 
  skills copied exactly from the Must_Have_Skills list in config — 
  never paraphrase, rename, or add skills not in that list

D2 EXPERIENCE RELEVANCE (0-10):
- Use evaluation_date from scoring_context as today's date when 
  calculating duration of "Present" roles
- Never assume "Present" means recently started
- Extract actual total years of FULL-TIME work from work history dates
- Apply Experience_Type from config:
    - FULLTIME_ONLY: count only full-time roles toward total years.
      Record internships separately in internship_summary as familiarity signals
    - INCLUDE_INTERNSHIPS: count all roles including internships toward total years
- Compare total against Experience_Required from config
- If candidate meets or exceeds requirement → no experience penalty
- If under by less than 25% → minor penalty (deduct 1-2 points)
- If under by 25-50% → moderate penalty (deduct 3-4 points)
- If under by more than 50% → significant penalty (deduct 5+ points)
- For Associate/Entry roles: relevant internships are strong industry familiarity
  signals — note them in internship_summary even under FULLTIME_ONLY
- Domain match: check against Primary_Industry AND Adjacent_Industries from config
  Full match = no penalty, adjacent match = minor penalty, no match = significant penalty

D3 SENIORITY ALIGNMENT (0-10):
- Compare candidate signals to Seniority_Level from config
- Signals: ownership language, scope of decisions, team size, budget ownership
- Exact match with strong evidence = 8-10
- One level off from required = 5-7
- Two levels off = 2-4

D4 IMPACT QUALITY (0-10):
- CAR method per bullet: Challenge + Action + Result = 3pts
- Quantified results with numbers or percentages = positive signal
- "Responsible for", "assisted", "helped" = negative signal
- Assess across all roles and projects, not just the most recent

D5 RED FLAG PENALTY (0 to 10, positive values only):
- Return a positive number representing penalty magnitude — code will negate it
- Employment gap over 6 months in full-time history = 2 per gap
- 3+ full-time jobs under 12 months = 4
- 1-2 full-time short stints under 12 months = 2
- Keyword stuffing = 2
- Overqualified by 2+ seniority levels = 2
- Internships = 0 penalty regardless of count or duration
- No issues = 0

OUTPUT FORMAT RULES — apply to every field in the JSON response:
- total_years_experience: max 5 words. e.g. "~10 months", "3.5 years". No sentences.
- graduation_year: 4 digit year only. e.g. "2025"
- is_student: "YES" or "NO" only
- current_company: company name only. e.g. "Media.net"
- total_years_found: same format as total_years_experience
- internship_summary: one line max. e.g. "2 APM internships — fintech, SaaS"
- domain_match: one line max. e.g. "Full match — Ad-Tech" or "Adjacent — SaaS, fintech"
- executive_summary: 2-3 sentences max, no bullet points
- key_evidence: 2-3 bullet points max, one line each
- reasoning fields: can be detailed, these are for internal review only

[ROLE CONFIGURATION]
${item.scoringConfig}

[RESUME TEXT]
${item.resumeText}

Return ONLY this exact JSON structure with no extra text:
{
  "candidate_summary": {
    "total_years_experience": "",
    "graduation_year": "",
    "is_student": "",
    "current_company": "",
    "executive_summary": ""
  },
  "d1_skills": {
    "score": 0,
    "reasoning": "",
    "evidence": "",
    "must_have_confirmed": [],
    "must_have_inferred": [],
    "must_have_missing": [],
    "nice_to_have_confirmed": [],
    "nice_to_have_inferred": []
  },
  "d2_experience": {
    "score": 0,
    "reasoning": "",
    "evidence": "",
    "total_years_found": "",
    "internship_summary": "",
    "domain_match": ""
  },
  "d3_seniority": {
    "score": 0,
    "reasoning": "",
    "evidence": ""
  },
  "d4_impact": {
    "score": 0,
    "reasoning": "",
    "evidence": ""
  },
  "d5_red_flags": {
    "score": 0,
    "flags": [],
    "reasoning": ""
  },
  "key_evidence": ""
}`
    }
  ]
};

return [{
  json: {
    requestBody: JSON.stringify(requestBody),
    candidateName: item.candidateName || '',
    candidateEmail: item.candidateEmail || '',
    candidateMobile: item.candidateMobile || '',
    candidateCurrentTitle: item.candidateCurrentTitle || '',
    candidateId: item.candidateId || '',
    fileId: item.fileId || '',
    fileName: item.fileName || '',
    submissionDate: item.submissionDate || '',
    config: item.config || {},
    inferredFields: item.inferredFields || '',
    resumeTruncated: item.resumeTruncated || false,
    confidencePenaltyFromExtraction: item.confidencePenaltyFromExtraction || 0,
    confidenceSignals: item.confidenceSignals || [],
    extractedMustHaveSkills: item.extractedMustHaveSkills || '',
    extractedNiceToHaveSkills: item.extractedNiceToHaveSkills || ''
  }
}];

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet

main.node: Append or update row in sheet
